channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABPZ20 KNHC 211720
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
1100 AM PDT Tue Jul 21 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Hurricane
Fausto, located several hundred miles southwest of the southern tip
of Baja California.

Eastern and Central East Pacific:
A broad area of low pressure is forecast to develop well south of
Mexico over the next couple of days. Environmental conditions
appear conducive for development of the system thereafter, and a
tropical depression is likely to form while it moves westward to
west-northwestward across the eastern and central portions of the
eastern Pacific.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...high...80 percent.

Southwest of Hawaii:
Showers and thunderstorms associated with a trough of low pressure
located well southwest of the Hawaiian Islands remain disorganized.
Environmental conditions appear conducive for some gradual
development of this system as it moves generally westward during the
next several days.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...low...30 percent.

$$
Forecaster Beven
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Hurricane Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...FAUSTO TURNS WEST-NORTHWESTWARD WHILE CHANGING LITTLE IN STRENGTH... As of 11:00 AM HST Tue Jul 21 the center of Fausto was located near 16.6, -119.9 with movement WNW at 12 mph. The minimum central pressure was 987 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 75 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Fausto
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...FAUSTO TURNS WEST-NORTHWESTWARD WHILE CHANGING LITTLE IN STRENGTH...

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Public Advisory Number 12
item.description:
Issued at 1100 AM HST Tue Jul 21 2026 
000
WTPZ31 KNHC 212033
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Hurricane Fausto Advisory Number  12
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 AM HST Tue Jul 21 2026
 
...FAUSTO TURNS WEST-NORTHWESTWARD WHILE CHANGING LITTLE IN
STRENGTH...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 1100 AM HST...2100 UTC...INFORMATION
-----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...16.6N 119.9W
ABOUT 780 MI...1260 KM WSW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...75 MPH...120 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...WNW OR 295 DEGREES AT 12 MPH...19 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...987 MB...29.15 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 1100 AM HST (2100 UTC), the center of Hurricane Fausto was
located near latitude 16.6 North, longitude 119.9 West. Fausto is
now moving toward the west-northwest near 12 mph (19 km/h). A 
general west-northwestward to westward motion is expected for the 
next several days.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 75 mph (120 km/h) with higher
gusts. Little change in strength is expected today. After that, 
gradual strengthening is expected tonight through Thursday.
 
Hurricane-force winds extend outward up to 30 miles (45 km) from the
center and tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 160 miles
(260 km).
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 987 mb (29.15 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF:  Swells generated by Fausto are expected to reach the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula tonight, and persist for a 
couple of days.  These swells are likely to cause life-threatening
surf and rip current conditions.  Please consult products from your
local weather office.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 500 PM HST.
 
$$
Forecaster Beven
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 12
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC TUE JUL 21 2026 
629 
WTPZ21 KNHC 212033
TCMEP1
 
HURRICANE FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  12
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
2100 UTC TUE JUL 21 2026
 
HURRICANE CENTER LOCATED NEAR 16.6N 119.9W AT 21/2100Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  20 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST-NORTHWEST OR 295 DEGREES AT  10 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  987 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  65 KT WITH GUSTS TO  80 KT.
64 KT....... 25NE  10SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT....... 60NE  60SE  20SW  40NW.
34 KT.......100NE 140SE 120SW  50NW.
4 M SEAS....240NE 210SE 120SW 150NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 16.6N 119.9W AT 21/2100Z
AT 21/1800Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 16.5N 119.5W
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0600Z 17.0N 121.1W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 25NE  15SE   0SW  20NW.
50 KT... 60NE  60SE  20SW  30NW.
34 KT...120NE 140SE 110SW  70NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1800Z 17.4N 122.7W
MAX WIND  70 KT...GUSTS  85 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  25SE  15SW  25NW.
50 KT... 60NE  60SE  30SW  40NW.
34 KT...130NE 130SE 110SW  90NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0600Z 17.7N 124.6W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 35NE  25SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 70NE  60SE  30SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 140SE 110SW 110NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1800Z 17.9N 127.0W
MAX WIND  80 KT...GUSTS 100 KT.
64 KT... 35NE  30SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 70NE  60SE  40SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 140SE 110SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0600Z 18.3N 129.6W
MAX WIND  85 KT...GUSTS 105 KT.
64 KT... 35NE  30SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 70NE  60SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 130SE 100SW 140NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1800Z 18.6N 132.3W
MAX WIND  85 KT...GUSTS 105 KT.
64 KT... 35NE  30SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 80NE  60SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...160NE 130SE 110SW 150NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 25/1800Z 19.4N 137.4W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  30SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 130SE 110SW 140NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 26/1800Z 20.1N 142.2W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
50 KT... 50NE  40SE  20SW  40NW.
34 KT...130NE 100SE  80SW 120NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 16.6N 119.9W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 22/0300Z
 
$$
FORECASTER BEVEN
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 12
item.description:
Issued at 1100 AM HST Tue Jul 21 2026 
000
WTPZ41 KNHC 212034
TCDEP1
 
Hurricane Fausto Discussion Number  12
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 AM HST Tue Jul 21 2026

The core of Fausto has not regained any organization since the 
previous advisory, and overall the satellite appearance is of a 
system below hurricane strength. However, a SAR overpass near 14Z 
indicated that the system still had hurricane-force winds, and this 
is supported by satellite intensity estimates from microwave 
sounders. Based on these, the initial intensity is held at 65 kt.

Fausto is starting its west-northwestward turn and the initial 
motion is now 295/10 kt.  The subtropical ridge to the north and 
northeast of the cyclone should build westward during the next 
five days, and this evolution should steer the cyclone west- 
northwestward to westward through the forecast period. As before, 
the track guidance remains tightly clustered for three days. After 
that, there remains an increase in the north-south spread between 
the GFS and Canadian models on the north side of the guidance 
envelope and the ECMWF, Google DeepMind, and the HAFS models on the 
south side of the guidance envelope.  For the first three days, the 
new forecast track lies near the previous forecast and the center of 
the guidance. At days 4 and 5, it is a little south of the previous 
track and a little north of the various consensus models.

While Fausto is forecast to be in a light- to moderate shear 
environment for the next several days, it will be moving over 
slowly decreasing sea surface temperatures through 72 h. 
Subsequently, the forecast track is near and roughly parallel to 
the 26C isotherm.  In addition, the atmospheric moisture near the 
storm should slowly decrease for 72 h, followed by a faster 
decrease. The intensity guidance responds to these factors by 
showing intensification for 48-60 h, with a gradual weakening after 
72 h. Based on the guidance and the need for the core to 
re-organize, the new intensity forecast shows little change in 
strength for 12 h, followed by gradual strengthening until the 60 h 
point. The forecast peak intensity of 85 kt is near the upper edge 
of the guidance. After 72 h, the forecast follows the overall 
weakening trend of the guidance. The new forecast is weaker than 
than the previous forecast through 60 h, and is similar to the 
previous forecast thereafter.

 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  21/2100Z 16.6N 119.9W   65 KT  75 MPH
 12H  22/0600Z 17.0N 121.1W   65 KT  75 MPH
 24H  22/1800Z 17.4N 122.7W   70 KT  80 MPH
 36H  23/0600Z 17.7N 124.6W   75 KT  85 MPH
 48H  23/1800Z 17.9N 127.0W   80 KT  90 MPH
 60H  24/0600Z 18.3N 129.6W   85 KT 100 MPH
 72H  24/1800Z 18.6N 132.3W   85 KT 100 MPH
 96H  25/1800Z 19.4N 137.4W   75 KT  85 MPH
120H  26/1800Z 20.1N 142.2W   65 KT  75 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Beven
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 12
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC TUE JUL 21 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 212033
PWSEP1
                                                                    
HURRICANE FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  12                
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
2100 UTC TUE JUL 21 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 2100Z THE CENTER OF HURRICANE FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR LATITUDE   
16.6 NORTH...LONGITUDE 119.9 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS NEAR 
65 KTS...75 MPH...120 KM/H.                                         
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        18Z TUE AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       18Z TUE 06Z WED 18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 18Z FRI 18Z SAT
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             06Z WED 18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 18Z FRI 18Z SAT 18Z SUN
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
15N 120W       34 95   X(95)   X(95)   X(95)   X(95)   X(95)   X(95)
 
15N 125W       34  X   X( X)   5( 5)   X( 5)   1( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)
 
20N 125W       34  X   X( X)  14(14)  11(25)   X(25)   X(25)   X(25)
 
15N 130W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
20N 130W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)  60(63)   1(64)   X(64)
20N 130W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  19(19)   X(19)   X(19)
20N 130W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)
 
15N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)   X( 3)
 
20N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  17(17)  58(75)   1(76)
20N 135W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)  38(40)   1(41)
20N 135W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  19(20)   X(20)
 
20N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  25(25)  40(65)
20N 140W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   7( 7)  26(33)
20N 140W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)  10(12)
 
25N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)
 
20N 145W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  19(19)
20N 145W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)
20N 145W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER BEVEN
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
